Class ViewAdminHome

java.lang.Object
guiAdminHome.ViewAdminHome
public class ViewAdminHome extends Object

Title: GUIAdminHomePage Class.

Description: The Java/FX-based Admin Home Page. This class provides the JavaFX GUI widgets that enable an admin to perform admin functions. This page contains a number of buttons that have not yet been implemented. What has been implemented may not work the way the final product requires and there maybe defects in this code. The class has been written using a singleton design pattern and is the View portion of the Model, View, Controller pattern. The pattern is designed that the all accesses to this page and its functions starts by invoking the static method displayAdminHome. No other method should attempt to instantiate this class as that is controlled by displayAdminHome. It ensure that only one instance of class is instantiated and that one is properly configured for each use. Please note that this implementation is not appropriate for concurrent systems with multiple users. This Baeldung article provides insight into the issues: https://www.baeldung.com/java-singleton

Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025

Version:
1.00 2025-08-17 Initial version, 1.01 2026-02-08 Changed theUser var to public
Author:
Lynn Robert Carter, Prince Dahiya

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • ViewAdminHome

      private ViewAdminHome()

      Method: GUIAdminHomePage()

      Description: This method initializes all the elements of the graphical user interface. This method determines the location, size, font, color, and change and event handlers for each GUI object. This is a singleton and is only performed once. Subsequent uses fill in the changeable fields using the displayAdminHome method.

  • Method Details

    • displayAdminHome

      public static void displayAdminHome(javafx.stage.Stage ps, User user)

      Method: displayAdminHome(Stage ps, User user)

      Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause the Admin Home page to be displayed. It first sets up every shared attributes so we don't have to pass parameters. It then checks to see if the page has been setup. If not, it instantiates the class, initializes all the static aspects of the GIUI widgets (e.g., location on the page, font, size, and any methods to be performed). After the instantiation, the code then populates the elements that change based on the user and the system's current state. It then sets the Scene onto the stage, and makes it visible to the user.

      Parameters:
      ps - specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
      user - specifies the User for this GUI and it's methods

    • setupLabelUI

      private void setupLabelUI(javafx.scene.control.Label l, String ff, double f, double w, javafx.geometry.Pos p, double x, double y)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
      Parameters:
      l - The Label object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the Button
      p - The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)

    • setupButtonUI

      private void setupButtonUI(javafx.scene.control.Button b, String ff, double f, double w, javafx.geometry.Pos p, double x, double y)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
      Parameters:
      b - The Button object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the Button
      p - The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)

    • setupTextUI

      private void setupTextUI(javafx.scene.control.TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, javafx.geometry.Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text input field
      Parameters:
      t - The TextField object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the Button
      p - The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)
      e - Is this TextField user editable?

    • setupComboBoxUI

      private void setupComboBoxUI(javafx.scene.control.ComboBox<String> c, String ff, double f, double w, double x, double y)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a ComboBox
      Parameters:
      c - The ComboBox object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the ComboBox
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)
